title: Customer Service Phone Sales
description:
Website: 
 



We’re Hiring:  

Customer Service / Sales Specialist (Full-Time, Remote – Independent Contractor) 

Company: AIMS Industrial – Sydney, Australia 

Location: Remote (Must reside within a 2-hour drive of Manila CBD) 

Schedule: Monday to Friday, Australian business hours Timeframe. 



Important: 

This is a strictly full-time role. Applicants who cannot commit to full-time hours or who live 

outside the 2-hour radius from Manila CBD will not be considered. 

About Us 

AIMS Industrial is a leading Australian supplier of industrial, maintenance, and safety 

products. We’re known for our quality range, reliable delivery, and professional customer 

service. As we continue to grow internationally, we’re expanding our dedicated support team 

in the Philippines to help maintain our high service standards. 



About the Role 

You’ll join our remote Manila-based team working closely with our Sydney head office. The 

role suits a reliable professional who thrives on customer interaction, values precision, and 

can manage multiple tasks with strong follow-up and accountability. You’ll also have 

autonomy to streamline processes and contribute to continuous improvement. 



Key Responsibilities 

• Handle customer communications via phone,
 and chat (Tawk). 

• Dealing with Shopify orders and Internal Jim System Process 

• Manage order inquiries, updates, cancellations, returns, and issue resolution. 

• Build ongoing customer relationships through consistent, high-quality service. 

• Prepare accurate and timely quotations while maintaining profitability. 

• Collaborate with suppliers on pricing, delivery, and order fulfillment. 

• Follow up on delayed orders, provide alternatives, and focus on customer retention. 

• Coordinate with internal departments (Product Data, Accounts, Marketing) to resolve 

issues and improve service. 

• Sourcing alternative Solutions. 

• Actively participate in supplier training and internal sales improvement programs.



What You’ll Need 

• Excellent written and spoken English communication skills. 

• Strong customer service focus and consistent follow-up habits. 

• Ability to complete tasks efficiently and meet deadlines. 

• Enthusiasm for learning about industrial products (engineering or trade background is 

a plus). 

• High attention to detail, strong organization, and multitasking ability. 

• A team-oriented mindset with a proactive problem-solving attitude. 

Technical Requirements 

• Internet Speed: 10Mbps Download Min Actual (Rec. Min. 25mb down and 5mb 

up)  

• Multicore Processor e.g. Intel i5 or higher; AMD Ryzen  

• Memory: 8GB  

• Hard Drive: 128GB  

• Operating System: Windows 10 or Mac OS Sequoia 



Why Join Us 

Our Manila team plays an essential role in our operations. You’ll receive structured training, 

ongoing support, and the opportunity to develop within an internationally growing company. 

This is not a call-Centre environment — it’s a professional, service-driven team that values 

long-term relationships and continuous improvement. 



Pay: Weekly, via AIMS Industrial.

title: Part-Time / Project-Based Draftsman or Architect
description:
Part-Time / Project-Based Draftsman or Architect

Company: Osborne Pro Builders / GorgeBuilders

Location: Remote (Must work in Eastern Time Zone)

Compensation: $300–$500 per completed drawing package

Work Volume: Approximately 1–2 projects per month (potential for growth)



Overview

Osborne Pro Builders designs and builds custom cabins in the Red River Gorge region of Kentucky. Many of these properties are short-term rental investments or private mountain retreats.

Every project begins with our Discovery & Design Package, which converts a client’s concept into build-ready drawings and a detailed construction cost analysis before construction begins. 

We are seeking a reliable draftsman or architect who can take project details and produce complete, construction-ready drawing packages suitable for estimating, engineering coordination, and permitting.

This role is project-based, typically 1–2 projects per month, with the potential for more as our build pipeline grows.



Long-Term Opportunity

We are not looking for a one-off drawing service.

We are looking for a long-term drafting partner to support our ongoing cabin builds in Kentucky.

Typical workload:

1–2 projects per month currently

Potential for increased volume as our build pipeline grows





Designs are typically:

500–1500 sq ft cabins

Deck-heavy mountain builds

Short-term rental focused layouts





If the relationship works well, this role can become consistent monthly work.



Core Responsibilities

Produce Construction-Ready Drawing Packages

Create complete drawing sets for custom residential cabins including:

Dimensioned floor plans

Exterior elevations

Roof plans

Deck layouts

Window and door schedules

Stair layouts

Basic structural layout for estimating





Drawings must be clear, accurate, and usable by builders and subcontractors for pricing and planning.



Translate Builder Concepts into Buildable Designs

Work directly with Osborne Pro Builders to convert project ideas into practical layouts.

Responsibilities include:

Translating rough sketches or concepts into construction drawings

Adjusting designs for terrain conditions common in Kentucky mountain builds

Designing decks, window placement, and layouts that maximize views

Revising drawings based on builder feedback







Produce Code-Appropriate Residential Drawings

Drawing packages must align with:

Kentucky residential construction expectations

U.S. residential building standards

Typical contractor and subcontractor requirements





Structural engineering stamps may be required depending on the project.



Required Diagram Sets

Each drawing package must include clear labeled diagrams that allow subcontractors to understand the project quickly.



Site Layout Diagram

Include:

Cabin footprint

Deck locations

Parking areas

Driveway approach

Utility entry points

Septic location (if known)

Well / water source (if known)

North arrow orientation







Floor Plan Diagram

Floor plans must clearly show:

Room labels

Interior dimensions

Door swings

Window placement

Appliance locations

Closet locations

Stair layouts

Ceiling heights where applicable







Roof Diagram

Include:

Roof pitch

Ridge lines

Valley locations

Total roof square footage

Linear footage of roofline

Overhang dimensions

Skylight locations (if applicable)







Exterior Elevation Diagrams

Provide all elevations:

Front

Rear

Left side

Right side





Include:

Finished floor height

Deck heights

Window and door locations

Exterior materials (siding, metal, stone, etc.)







Deck Layout Diagram

Include:

Deck square footage

Covered vs uncovered areas

Stair locations

Railing linear footage

Structural post layout where visible







Mechanical System Layout Requirements

Drawings must include basic mechanical layout indicators sufficient for budgeting and construction planning.



Plumbing Layout

Indicate locations for:

Toilets

Showers / tubs

Bathroom sinks

Kitchen plumbing

Laundry plumbing (if applicable)

Water heater location





Provide counts for:

Toilets

Showers / tubs

Sinks

Plumbing penetrations

Gas pipe penetrations







HVAC Layout

Identify:

HVAC system location

Mechanical closet (if applicable)

Mini-split or HVAC unit locations

Primary ventilation areas





Final HVAC engineering may be handled separately.



Electrical Layout

Indicate locations for:

Interior light fixtures

Exterior light fixtures

Electrical panel (if known)

Major appliance connections





Also note low-voltage systems if included:

Audio systems

Security systems

Smart home wiring







Water Heating

Specify:

Tank or on-demand water heater

Water heater location

GPM rating if on-demand

Distance from electrical panel to heater (if known)







Required Quantity Data for Budgeting

The draftsman must also provide basic quantity information from the drawings so the builder can complete the project budget spreadsheet.



Design Elements

Number of bedrooms

Number of bathrooms

Total square footage

Total roof square footage

Linear roofline footage

Stair railing linear footage

Total siding square footage

Linear footage of baseboard / casing







Exterior

Custom exterior entry door

Exterior wall / painted surface square footage

Exterior doors

Specialty exterior door knobs

Skylights / sun tubes

Covered deck square footage

Deck square footage







Interior Finish Quantities

Provide quantities for:

Paintable wall square footage

Garage paint wall square footage

Tongue & groove surface square footage

Drywall square footage

Interior doors

Barn doors

Window count

Closet count





Flooring quantities:

LVP / wood flooring

Tile flooring

Carpet flooring







Plumbing Fixtures

Provide counts for:

Toilets

Showers / tubs

Sinks

Countertop square footage

Cabinets (kitchen and bath)

Plumbing holes

Gas pipe penetrations







Electrical Fixtures

Interior light fixtures

Exterior light fixtures

Low voltage elements







Required Skills

Proficiency in at least one:

AutoCAD

Revit

Chief Architect

SketchUp with construction documentation tools





Ability to produce clear, dimensioned residential construction drawings.



Preferred Experience

Strong preference for candidates with experience in:

Residential cabin design

Mountain or hillside construction

Short-term rental property design

Deck-heavy architectural layouts







Work Requirements

Must:

Work during Eastern Time business hours

Be responsive to revision requests

Communicate clearly with builders

Deliver drawings in a timely manner







Deliverables Per Project

Each project must include:

Complete drawing package (PDF)

Editable CAD/design files

Labeled diagrams and elevations

Mechanical layout indicators

Quantity takeoff information for budgeting

Revision support until drawings are finalized







Compensation

Per completed drawing package:

$300 – $500 depending on project complexity

Typical project size:

500–1500 square foot cabins

Deck-focused mountain designs

STR-friendly layouts







Ideal Candidate

We are looking for someone who:

Thinks like a builder

Understands practical construction methods

Produces clean, organized drawings

Values accuracy and clarity





Our goal is clear drawings that eliminate guesswork and allow accurate project pricing.



Application Instructions

To help us filter serious applicants, please follow this instruction when applying.

Start your application message with the word:

CABIN

Applications that do not include this word at the beginning will not be reviewed.

Please also include:

Sample drawings or portfolio

Software used

Typical turnaround time

Confirmation you can work in Eastern Time

title: B2B Cold Caller / Appointment Setter (Fleet Outreach – DOT Medical Exams) – Trial Project
description:
Job Overview



We are looking for a confident B2B cold caller / appointment setter to help us contact companies and schedule onsite DOT medical exam days for commercial drivers.



This role will begin as a short-term pilot project (3–4 weeks) to test an outbound outreach system.



If the campaign performs well and exam days are being scheduled successfully, there will be an opportunity to continue in a long-term role with increased hours and bonuses.



?



About Us



We provide FMCSA-compliant DOT medical examinations for commercial drivers and specialize in onsite fleet certification days.



Instead of sending drivers individually to clinics, companies can schedule a driver certification day so all drivers complete their DOT physicals onsite in a few hours.



Our service helps companies:



• keep drivers compliant

• reduce driver downtime

• simplify DOT compliance



We work with companies that employ CDL drivers across the Orlando area.



?



Your Responsibilities



You will be provided with a list of companies and will:



• Call companies to determine if they employ CDL drivers

• Ask for the fleet manager / safety manager / HR manager

• Introduce our onsite DOT medical exam services

• Determine the number of drivers in the fleet

• Send follow-up
s when requested

• Schedule fleet exam days on our calendar

• Track outreach and follow-ups in a spreadsheet



The goal is to schedule fleet exam days for companies with 10–30 drivers.



?



Expected Activity



• 80–120 calls per day

• 400–600 calls per week

• Identify companies with CDL drivers

• Schedule fleet exam days when interest is confirmed



?



Project Duration



Initial pilot project:



3–4 weeks.



If the outreach campaign is successful and exam days are being scheduled consistently, the position may transition into a long-term role with additional hours and bonus incentives.



?



Requirements



We are specifically looking for someone who has experience in:



• B2B cold calling

• appointment setting

• lead generation

• sales development (SDR)



You must have:



• strong spoken English

• confident phone presence

• reliable internet connection

• quiet work environment



Experience calling US businesses is strongly preferred.



Call center experience is a plus.



?



Compensation



Hourly compensation will be discussed with candidates based on experience and expectations.



Performance bonuses will also be available for successfully scheduled fleet exam days.



Estimated hours during pilot phase:



20–30 hours per week.



?



Important Instructions for Applicants



To be considered, please include the following:



A 30-second voice recording introducing yourself.



A short written answer to this question:



“How would you approach a company to schedule an onsite DOT medical exam day for their drivers?”



Include the word “COMPLIANCE” at the beginning of your application so we know you read the full job description.



Applications without these requirements will not be considered.



?



Interview Process



Shortlisted candidates will be asked to:



• participate in audio interview

• complete a 5-minute mock call scenario



?



Interview Questions



Candidates who reach the interview stage will be asked questions such as:



“How do you handle a situation where the person answering the phone says they are not interested and tries to end the call quickly?”



“What questions would you ask to determine whether a company is a good candidate for scheduling a fleet exam day?”



“Tell us about a time you successfully booked appointments through cold calling.”



?



Final Step



When applying, please also tell us:



“What hourly rate are you expecting for this role?”

title: Experienced Accountant
description:
Accountant (Remote)



About Us

We’re on a mission to bring high-quality, air- and oven-dried meat snacks to consumers across the U.S. through our growing portfolio of brands. As pioneers in the American biltong space, we take pride in offering protein-packed, clean-label snacks made with simple and honest ingredients.

As our operations continue to expand and the company grows, we are looking for a reliable and experienced Accountant to join our team and support the company’s finance and accounting operations.



Position Overview

We are seeking a detail-oriented and proactive Accountant with diverse accounting experience who can assist with day-to-day accounting tasks and support the preparation of financial reports. The ideal candidate is comfortable working with different accounting functions such as payroll, accounts receivable, accounts payable, and financial statement preparation.

This role is ideal for someone who is organized, analytical, and capable of managing multiple accounting responsibilities in a remote work environment.



Key Responsibilities

• Handle daily accounting operations, including recording transactions and maintaining accurate financial records

• Manage Accounts Receivable, including invoicing customers and monitoring collections

• Process and manage Accounts Payable, ensuring timely and accurate payments to vendors

• Assist with payroll processing and payroll-related entries

• Perform account reconciliations and ensure balances are accurate

• Assist in the preparation of financial statements and other financial reports

• Maintain organized and complete supporting documentation for financial transactions

• Work with the team to ensure accounting records are accurate and up to date

• Assist in improving accounting processes and maintaining proper financial controls





Qualifications

• Certified Public Accountant (CPA) required

• Experience with accounting software or ERP systems; familiarity with Sage 300 is an advantage

• Solid understanding of general accounting principles and financial reporting

• Experience handling multiple accounting functions such as payroll, A/R, and A/P

• Strong attention to detail and organizational skills

• Ability to work independently in a remote setup

• Good communication skills and ability to collaborate with team members



Work Setup

• Remote work

• 3:00 PM – 12:00 AM Philippine Time

 Reels.

title: Business Developer
description:
Job Developer – Mental Health Services



The Job Developer is responsible for building relationships with organizations to expand access to mental health support for employees. This role focuses on outreach to companies, community partners, and HR departments to introduce and promote our clinic’s services, including Employee Assistance Programs (EAP), workplace workshops, and drop-in counselling.



Key responsibilities include identifying and contacting potential partner organizations, presenting our mental health services to employers, developing referral pathways, and coordinating partnerships that support employee wellbeing. The Job Developer will also collaborate with the clinical team to ensure services meet workplace needs and help design programs such as stress management workshops, mental health training, and accessible counselling options for staff.



The ideal candidate is proactive, relationship-focused, and passionate about increasing access to mental health care in the workplace.

title: Creative Ghostwriter: Nonfiction Manifesto with Sci-Fi Epic Tone.
description:
I am looking for a writer to dramatize the mission of living to 120 and beyond. You must be familiar with movie epics like The Matrix and Troy.  I provide the data; you provide the cinematic world-building.



You are a writer comfortable with a hybrid workflow. I provide the core mission data and 'mindset shifts.'  You will use AI tools (Claude, Sudowrite, etc.) to generate the high-volume narrative prose, while your primary value will be in the 'Director’s Cut'—ensuring the tone matches movie epics like The Matrix and is not AI drivel.



This 5,000-word paid test pilot (approx. 1 week of work) from 3 candidates will lead to the final selection of the ghost writer for the entire book.

title: VIDEO EDITOR FOR AI & TECH YOUTUBE CHANNEL. FULL-TIME, LONG-TERM POSITION
description:
VIDEO EDITOR FOR AI & TECH YOUTUBE CHANNEL. FULL-TIME, LONG-TERM POSITION



We're a growing YouTube channel covering AI, automation, robotics, and emerging technology. We're hiring 2 dedicated video editors for full-time, long-term positions.



This is not a side gig or one-off freelance project. We're building something real, and we're looking for editors who want to grow with the channel long-term. If you're early in your career, hungry to build a serious portfolio, and ready to commit to consistent, high-volume work, this is your opportunity to get in on the ground floor of a channel that's scaling fast. The editors who prove themselves here will have a clear path to expanded roles, higher pay, and real ownership over production.





WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING



Editing 3 long-form YouTube videos per week (9 to 22 minutes each) plus 1 vertical short (30 to 35 seconds). That's 4 deliverables per week. Each long-form video follows a detailed Editor's Table, a scene-by-scene visual blueprint that tells you exactly what goes on screen at every timecode. Every shot type, every B-roll asset, every visual composition, every documentation link is mapped out for you. You'll never have to guess what the vision is. Your job is to execute it at a high level with creative energy, polished visuals, and dynamic pacing.



Each video requires a visual change every 5 seconds. Zooms, cuts, motion graphics, text animations, transitions, split-screen compositions, kinetic text moments, and data overlays. This is fast-paced, high-retention editing designed to keep viewers locked in.



The vertical short is pulled from the best clip across your 3 long-form videos. After probation, this increases to 1 short per video (3 vertical shorts per week).





BREAKING NEWS PRIORITY



We also cover breaking AI news. When a major story drops, you may need to reprioritize your current workload and turn around a breaking news video as fast as possible. This means pausing whatever you're working on and editing the urgent video first. Speed and flexibility matter here. If you can't handle shifting priorities on short notice, this role isn't for you.





WHAT'S PROVIDED VS. WHAT YOU BUILD



Provided by us:



All A-roll footage (AI avatar talking head). You do NOT film or source A-roll.



An Editor's Handbook, your permanent reference guide covering every production standard, shot type definition, B-roll category, branded glitch placement, hook rules, avatar cleanup pipeline, and quality benchmarks. This is your go-to document for how things work on this channel. You'll receive it on day one.



A detailed Editor's Table for every video. A scene-by-scene visual blueprint with exact timecodes, shot types, visual assignments, B-roll tags, Director's Notes, and full spoken dialogue for every scene. You'll never have to guess what the vision is.



Documentation links and screenshot instructions. You capture the screenshots.



Stock footage keywords. You pull the clips from Envato Elements (access included).



After Effects composition directives. You build the compositions from detailed descriptions specifying elements, layout, colors, and animation.



YouTube source video search terms. You source real footage clips using WayinVideo (wayin.ai), a tool that lets you search within YouTube videos to find specific moments.



Quote card text and sources. You design and place them.



Full-screen kinetic text lines. You create bold, impactful typography moments.



Digital Twin Intro clips. Pre-recorded footage for the channel's signature intro sequence that appears at the start of every video. You'll receive the raw clips and build the intro following the Editor's Table direction.



What you build:



You're not sourcing content or coming up with ideas. Everything is mapped out for you in the Editor's Table, and the Editor's Handbook is your permanent reference for standards. But you ARE building the visuals, compositions, and motion graphics that bring it to life. Your creative judgment matters. How you cut, how you pace, how you build visual energy between scenes is your craft. The Editor's Table is the blueprint. You bring it to life.





THE 40/60 SPLIT



About 40% of each video features the AI avatar on screen. The remaining 60% is full-screen B-roll: custom After Effects compositions, documentation screenshots, source video footage, stock footage, quote cards, and kinetic text. A visual change must happen every 5 seconds. No static frames.





AVATAR SHOT TYPES YOU'LL WORK WITH



You'll work with five shot types that rotate throughout every video:



Full Frame. Pure avatar, direct to camera. Maximum 5 to 6 seconds per clip. Used sparingly at scene endings for emotional payoff.



Split Panel. Avatar on one side (~40% of frame), data/documentation/visual on the other (~60%). This is the primary delivery mode. The panel content changes every 5 seconds.



With Overlay. Avatar visible but kinetic text, stat bars, or lower thirds appear on screen. The viewer's eye is drawn to the data. Maximum 2 per scene.



Zoom Punch. Quick 1 to 2 second tight crop cut on the avatar's face for punchlines and pivots. Target 6 to 8 per video minimum.



PIP Float. Small circular avatar cutout (~30 to 35% of frame) floating over full-screen source footage. Features an orbiting glow trail around the circle edge. Used when real YouTube footage plays underneath the avatar's voice. Target 5 to 7 per video. Can run longer than other shot types (8 to 15 seconds).





B-ROLL TYPES YOU'LL WORK WITH



Every visual in the Editor's Table is tagged with one of six categories:



B.R1, Documentation: Full-screen screenshot of a source page, article, report, or data dashboard. You open the provided link, screenshot the relevant section, and drop it in. This is proof, showing the viewer real evidence.



B.R2, Authority Quote: Quote card overlay attributed to a specific person. For recognizable public figures, include a photo alongside the quote. 0 to 3 per video.



B.R3, AE Composition: Custom After Effects motion graphics. Headline stacks, timelines, comparison charts, data visualizations, ecosystem maps. You build these from detailed written directives. Maximum 8 per video.



B.R4, Stock Footage: Atmospheric footage from Envato Elements. Used for mood and energy, not proof. Short clips only, 3 to 5 seconds maximum.



B.R5, Kinetic Text: Full-screen animated text moments for key phrases and thesis statements, or lower third name/title/stat bars. Maximum 12 per video.



B.R6a, Source Video: Real YouTube footage sourced via WayinVideo. The Editor's Table provides search terms. You search YouTube for the title, paste the URL into WayinVideo, use "Find Moments" with the provided keywords, select the best clip, and trim. Visual only, always mute source audio. Minimum 8 per video. A visible channel credit 
 must appear on screen for every source video clip.





BRANDED GLITCH (SOUND IDENTITY)



Every video features 4 branded glitches, a signature chromatic RGB split with a digital glitch sound effect, lasting 0.3 seconds. These are the ONLY sound effects in the entire video. No additional SFX on stat reveals, transitions, scene changes, or anywhere else. The branded glitch is the sound identity of the channel. Placement is specified in the Editor's Table.





LIVE-ACTION VIDEOS (OCCASIONAL)



The majority of your work will be AI avatar videos. However, there will be occasions where you'll edit videos featuring the real Francis on camera. For example, hands-on product reviews, studio walkthroughs, or special topic deep dives. These videos still follow the same B-roll editing standards (documentation screenshots, source footage, AE compositions, kinetic text) but with real human footage as the A-roll instead of the avatar. The AI cleanup pipeline does not apply to these videos. Frequency will vary, but expect this as part of the role.





AI AVATAR CLEANUP (CRITICAL)



The AI avatar footage contains synthesis artifacts (temporal flickering, face-body boundary distortion, micro-jitter, and compression ghosting) that are visible on large screens and monitors. Before any editing begins, all avatar A-roll must go through this cleanup pipeline:



1. After Effects, Neat Video: Apply to all avatar A-roll. Reduces temporal flickering and compression artifacts. This is the primary tool for cleaning AI avatar footage.



2. After Effects, RSMB (ReelSmart Motion Blur): Add motion blur between frames. Smooths the micro-jitter that causes the "dizzy" feeling on AI faces.



3. After Effects, Mocha Face Tracking: Track the face region, apply subtle skin noise reduction to face only. Do not over-smooth. Keep it natural.



4. After Effects, Face-Body Seam Cleanup: Gaussian blur 0.5 to 1px on the boundary where AI face meets body/neck. Hides the synthesis seam.



5. Premiere Pro, Color Grade: Consistent Lumetri color grade across all avatar shots. Match color temperature with B-roll.



6. Premiere Pro, Film Grain Overlay: Organic film grain at 5 to 10% opacity over entire video. Grain masks digital artifacts and makes footage feel natural.



You must also be able to identify and mask unnatural AI movements, such as awkward hand gestures or slow, robotic eye blinks, through strategic cuts, zooms, B-roll placement, and motion graphics. The goal is to never let the viewer notice something is off.



If you've worked with AI-generated footage before (HeyGen, Synthesia, or similar), that's a big plus. If not, you must be willing to learn these techniques quickly.





REQUIRED SKILLS



These are non-negotiable. If you can't demonstrate these in your portfolio or test project, this role isn't the right fit.



Adobe Premiere Pro. Confident, daily-driver level.



Adobe After Effects. Must be comfortable building custom compositions from written directives (animated diagrams, comparison layouts, data visualizations, framework graphics, headline stacks, timelines).



Split-screen composition. Building and managing dynamic split-panel layouts where content changes every 5 seconds.



Motion graphics and kinetic text. Bold full-screen typography, animated stat callouts, lower thirds, text reveals.



Color grading. Lumetri color correction, skin tone matching, consistent grade across mixed footage sources.



Noise reduction and artifact cleanup. Neat Video or equivalent temporal noise reduction experience.



Face tracking. Mocha or similar tool for selective face region processing.



AI footage awareness. Ability to spot and fix synthesis artifacts (ghosting, jitter, face-body seam, unnatural movement) through cleanup tools and strategic editing choices.





OTHER REQUIREMENTS



A computer capable of handling 4K video editing.

Reliable internet and consistent availability.

Strong attention to detail.

Ability to meet weekly deadlines (3 long-form videos + 1 short per week).

Comfortable using Asana for task management and team communication. All assignments, feedback, and deadlines are managed through Asana.





COMPENSATION AND GROWTH



$1,000 USD per month paid bi-weekly ($500 every two weeks). This is probationary pay for the first 90 days.



The $1,000 monthly rate is based on delivering all 16 videos per month (12 long-form + 4 shorts). Pay is tied to output. If deliverables are consistently missed or incomplete, compensation will be adjusted accordingly.



We cover the Upwork service fee. You receive the full $1,000.



Guaranteed pay increase after 90 days as long as quality remains high and you pass probation.



Full-time, long-term position with room to grow.



Envato Elements access provided for stock footage and assets.



Entry-level candidates and recent graduates welcome. We value reliability and hunger over years of experience.





POST-PROBATION GROWTH (AFTER 90 DAYS)



This is where things get exciting. After probation, top performers take on expanded responsibilities and higher pay. This includes:



YouTube thumbnail creation (templates and direction provided).

Full YouTube publishing: uploading, metadata, thumbnails, scheduling.

Script development and content creation support.

End-to-end video production ownership.

1 short per video (3 vertical shorts per week, up from 1).



Expanded responsibilities come with increased pay. The more you grow, the more you earn. We want editors who see this as a career path, not just a paycheck.





WORKING WITH US. WHAT TO EXPECT



This is a professional working relationship and we want to be upfront about how things work so there are no surprises.



Revisions and Quality Control:

Every video goes through a review process before publishing. You should expect feedback and revision requests on most edits, especially in the first few months as we align on style and standards. Revisions are a normal part of the workflow, not a sign that something went wrong. If something doesn't meet our quality standards, you'll be asked to fix it before it goes live. The goal is to get better together over time.



Confidentiality:

All scripts, workflows, production systems, visual templates, and content strategies you're exposed to through this role are confidential. You may not share, reproduce, or discuss any internal materials, processes, or unreleased content outside of this working relationship. This applies during and after your time with us.



Work Ownership:

All content you create in this role (edits, compositions, motion graphics, templates, shorts, thumbnails) belongs to the channel. You may not reuse, repurpose, or include any work created for us in your personal portfolio or for other clients without written permission.



Availability and Communication:

You're expected to be reachable during agreed working hours via Asana. All assignments, deadlines, feedback, and revisions are managed through Asana, not
, not DMs. When breaking news hits, same-day communication and turnaround may be required. If you're unavailable for an extended period, advance notice is expected.



Publishing Authorization (Post-Probation):

Once you're granted access to the YouTube channel for publishing, you may only upload, schedule, or modify content that has been explicitly approved. No unauthorized uploads, title changes, description edits, or scheduling adjustments without sign-off.





PERFORMANCE STANDARDS AND CONTRACTOR TERMS



This is an independent contractor arrangement through Upwork. This is not an employment relationship and does not include benefits, severance, or employment protections.



During the 90-day probation period, either party may end the arrangement at any time, for any reason, with no notice required.



After probation, we follow a structured feedback process. If quality standards are not being met, deadlines are consistently missed, or communication breaks down, the process is:



1. Verbal feedback identifying the specific issue and what needs to change.

2. Written notice via Asana with a clear improvement timeline.

3. If the issue persists after written notice, the arrangement will be ended.



We are direct about expectations and fair about giving you the chance to improve. That said, certain actions may result in immediate termination without the above process, including but not limited to: breach of confidentiality, unauthorized publishing or modification of channel content, extended unexcused absence without notice, or any conduct that puts the channel's reputation or operations at risk.



We reserve the right to end the arrangement at our discretion. Reasonable notice will be provided whenever possible.



This is designed to be a long-term home. We're not looking to cycle through editors. We want people who grow with us, take ownership, and build something they're proud of. If you bring the work ethic and talent, we'll bring the opportunity, structure, and pay increases to match.





TO APPLY



Link to your portfolio or 2 to 3 sample edits.

Brief description of your editing setup (computer specs, software).

Your availability and timezone.

Short note on why you'd be a good fit.



We're looking for someone reliable, detail-oriented, and excited to grow with a channel focused on cutting-edge AI content. This is a full-time commitment, ideal for someone early in their career who wants consistent work, a growing portfolio, and a long-term home.

title: Operations & Research Assistant
description:
Type: Full-time, Permanent

Location: Remote / Work From Home



Pay: Php30,000 – Php40,000 per month (depending on experience)



?



Work Schedule (PST – California Time)



• 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM PST (most of the time)

• Flexibility required based on the Executive’s schedule



?

Hiring Process



Our hiring process includes the following steps:



1. Assessment

Complete the following assessment to expedite the review process:





2. Initial Interview



3. Final Interview



Candidates who complete the assessment early may be prioritized for interview scheduling.



-------------

Application Requirement



Please include a link to your resume/CV when applying.



In your message, briefly describe a situation where you conducted research and presented options or recommendations for a decision.



Applications without this information will not be considered.



?



About the Role



We are seeking a highly analytical Operations & Research Associate to work closely with the Owner/Executive on research-driven initiatives and operational projects.



This role is not a traditional administrative position. Instead, you will be responsible for researching solutions, evaluating vendors, organizing complex information, and preparing structured proposals that support executive decision-making.



You will work on a wide range of projects — from identifying vendors and tools to preparing detailed comparison reports and coordinating operational initiatives.



?



Key Responsibilities



Research & Analysis



• Conduct in-depth research on vendors, markets, tools, and business initiatives

• Identify potential vendors and gather pricing, capabilities, and service information

• Prepare comparison reports, proposals, and decision-ready summaries

• Organize findings into clear, structured recommendations

• Develop briefing materials for executive review and strategic discussions



?



Operations & Project Management



• Support operational initiatives and special projects from planning to execution

• Track deadlines, milestones, and project deliverables

• Coordinate with internal teams and external vendors

• Proactively follow up to ensure progress and completion of tasks

• Build and maintain systems to track projects, priorities, and action items



?



Executive Project Support



• Prepare reports, presentations, and memorandums

• Organize and maintain documentation and project records

• Assist with evaluating business tools, vendors, and operational improvements

• Anticipate project needs and proactively solve problems



?



Qualifications



• Strong research and analytical skills

• Experience conducting research and organizing findings into reports or proposals

• Demonstrated project management or coordination experience

• Excellent written and verbal communication skills

• Real estate experience is a strong plus

title: Interior Designer
description:
Senior Interior Designer (3-Month Contract) – Sydney Design Firm

About the Role:

We are a Sydney-based interior design company looking for a highly skilled offshore Interior Designer to join our team for a fixed 3-month contract. We need someone who can hit the ground running, work autonomously, and produce high-quality technical and creative outputs.

Technical Requirements:

• Software Proficiency: Expert level in AutoCAD, SketchUp, or Rhino.

• Documentation: Ability to produce highly detailed technical drawings and construction sets.

• Schedules: Experienced in creating comprehensive finishes schedules and specifying materials.

Creative Requirements:

• Concept Design: Able to develop unique design concepts from a brief.

• Mood Boards: High-level presentation skills for client-facing mood boards.

• Materiality: Strong understanding of textures, finishes, and color palettes.

Work Style:

• Autonomous: Must be able to manage tasks with minimal supervision.

• Efficient: Capable of meeting tight deadlines without sacrificing detail.

• Communication: Fluent English; able to overlap at least 5 hours with Sydney (AEST) business hours.

To Apply:

Please send your CV and a link to your Portfolio. Your portfolio must include examples of 3D renders, technical drawing sets, and a sample finishes schedule. Please include your expected monthly salary in AUD.

title: Junior Graphic Designer for Las Vegas Fashion & Retail
description:
About the Role



We are looking for a versatile Junior Graphic Designer who is passionate about visual storytelling and capable of producing high-quality creative assets across multiple platforms.



This role is ideal for a designer who enjoys working across
 campaigns, web graphics, social media visuals, and print collateral, while collaborating closely with marketing and creative teams. The successful candidate will be comfortable working with modern design tools, including Figma, Adobe Creative Cloud, Miro, and AI-assisted creative tools, and will have a strong eye for layout, typography, and brand consistency.





Key Responsibilities:

-Design and produce
 campaign layouts and graphics optimized for modern
 platforms.

-Create web and digital marketing assets, including banners, landing page visuals, and promotional graphics.

-Develop engaging social media graphics tailored for multiple platforms.

-Produce print-ready marketing materials such as brochures, flyers, posters, and promotional collateral.

-Work collaboratively with team members using Figma and Miro for design planning, ideation, and workflow coordination.

-Use AI-assisted creative tools to support concept development, design production, and workflow efficiency.

-Maintain visual consistency with brand guidelines across all deliverables.

-Manage multiple projects simultaneously while meeting deadlines in a fast-paced environment.

-Contribute creative ideas and design solutions to support marketing campaigns and brand initiatives.



Required Skills:

-Proficiency in Figma for layout, digital design, and collaborative workflows.

-Strong working knowledge of Adobe Creative Cloud (Photoshop, Illustrator, InDesign).

-Experience creating
 campaign graphics, social media content, web assets, and print materials.

-Familiarity with Miro or other collaborative whiteboarding tools.

-Exposure to AI-powered creative tools used in modern design workflows.

-Strong understanding of layout, typography, color theory, and visual hierarchy.

-Ability to take creative direction and execute designs efficiently.



Preferred Qualifications:

-1–3 years of professional design experience.

-Familiarity with
 marketing platforms (Mailchimp, Klaviyo, HubSpot, etc.).

-Basic understanding of UI/UX design principles.

-Experience working within marketing or creative teams.



What We Value:

-We are looking for someone who is:

-Curious and eager to learn new tools and technologies

-Detail-oriented with a strong sense of design quality

-Able to balance creativity with strategic marketing objectives

-Comfortable working in a collaborative, fast-paced creative environment



Portfolio Requirement:

-Applicants must provide a portfolio demonstrating strong design fundamentals and work in at least several of the following areas:


 campaign design

-Social media graphics

-Web or digital marketing assets

-Print materials

title: VSL Video Editor – Direct Response / Skincare (Long-Form Sales Videos)
description:
MUST HAVE EXPERIENCE WITH VSL's ALREADY



Also must be good with using AI



Most importantly send google drive of previous work as well, thanks.



-----------------------------



We are a skincare brand building high-converting Video Sales Letters (VSLs) for a product targeting women 30+ dealing with tretinoin skin damage.

We need a video editor who understands direct response video — not just "making things look pretty." This is about pacing, tension, emotional rhythm, and keeping a viewer watching for 15–30+ minutes.

What you'll be editing:



Long-form VSLs (15–45 minutes) built from UGC-style clips, screen recordings, and text-on-screen sequences

Short-form hooks and ads (15–60 seconds) pulled from the longer VSL

Testimonial compilation clips

Before/after sequences with proper pacing



What we need you to know (or learn fast):



How to pace a VSL so it doesn't lose the viewer at the 3-minute mark

How to use text overlays, zooms, cuts, and transitions that serve the STORY — not just look flashy

How to edit UGC footage to feel authentic but polished

How to sync voiceover/narration with b-roll and text

Basic color correction and audio cleanup

CapCut, Premiere Pro, or DaVinci Resolve



Bonus (not required but will move you to the top):



You've edited VSLs, advertorials, or long-form direct response video before

You understand what a "hook," "lead," "mechanism," and "close" mean in a sales video

You've worked in health, beauty, or skincare niches

You can show examples of long-form sales videos you've edited (not music videos, not vlogs — sales videos)

title: Operations Coordinator - U.S. Business | PHP 30K-37K | Long-Term Role
description:
Established U.S. business seeking an experienced Operations Coordinator to support tax, real estate, and lending operations. 



This is a client-facing role requiring strong phone presence and high-level organization. You will serve as the central coordination point across multiple business lines under one owner. 



Schedule: Monday–Friday | 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM EST | 1-hour unpaid lunch



Professionalism, maturity, and strong follow-through are essential.



This is a long-term position for someone seeking stability and growth.

Not entry-level.



CORE RESPONSIBILITIES

Answer and return client phone calls professionally

Follow up with tax, real estate, and lending clients

Manage inbox and calendars

Coordinate appointments and document collection

Maintain organized Google Drive systems

Update CRM and internal trackers

Keep daily operations structured and moving



You will support multiple business functions. The ability to manage shifting priorities calmly and professionally is critical.



REQUIREMENTS (NON-NEGOTIABLE)

2+ years experience as VA or office administrator

Strong experience handling U.S. client phone calls

Clear, confident spoken English

Highly organized and detail-oriented

Quiet workspace

Stable high-speed internet (minimum 50 Mbps)

Reliable equipment (dual monitors preferred)

Able to work 9AM–6PM EST consistently

Seeking long-term employment



If you frequently change jobs, this role is not a fit.



COMPENSATION

Salary: PHP 30,000 – 37,000 per month (based on experience)



Performance reviews at 30 and 90 days.

Salary increases available for strong performance and reliability.



HOW TO APPLY

To be considered, send:

1. Updated resume

2. A 1–2 minute video recording introducing yourself (camera on, professional setting)

3. Screenshot of internet speed test

4. Short paragraph explaining why you are seeking long-term stability



In your video, please introduce yourself and describe your experience handling U.S. client phone calls.



Please also answer: What is the longest job you have held while working remotely, and how long did you stay there?



Subject line: Client-Facing Admin – EST Ready



Incomplete applications will not be reviewed.

title: isputes & Chargebacks Team Lead (Stripe / PayPal / Shopify Payments) — Remote (Full-Time)
description:
PLEASE READ CAREFULLY 



We’re hiring an experienced Disputes & Chargebacks Team Lead to own and optimize our chargeback/dispute operations across multiple payment processors. You will manage day-to-day case strategy, improve SOPs, lead a small team, and drive measurable improvements in win rate and dispute ratio.



You’ll be responsible for keeping our payment operations healthy, reducing financial loss, and building repeatable systems that scale.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

1) Disputes Execution & Strategy



Handle chargebacks/disputes end-to-end across Stripe, PayPal, Shopify Payments (and other processors when needed)



Build strong evidence packages: order proof, tracking proof, customer communication logs, product pages, policies, IP/device data (when available), etc.



Ensure on-time submissions and compliance with each processor’s timelines and rules



Write high-quality rebuttals and structured dispute responses that improve win rates



2) Process & SOP Ownership



Build and maintain private SOPs per processor (Stripe vs PayPal vs Shopify Payments rules differ)



Create playbooks by dispute reason code (fraud, not received, not as described, refund not processed, etc.)



Implement standard templates, tagging rules, escalation flows, and evidence checklists



3) Analytics & Performance Management



Produce weekly reporting: win rate, dispute ratio, top dispute reasons, loss drivers, recurring patterns



Identify root causes (shipping delays, unclear product expectations, policy gaps, CX quality issues) and coordinate fixes with the CX/Operations team



Continuously improve KPIs and prevent repeat dispute patterns



4) Team Leadership (Hands-on)



Hire support/disputes agents (Philippines preferred) and train them



Run quality control: audit cases, correct mistakes, coach agents, and enforce SOP compliance



Create training materials and ensure consistent dispute handling at scale



STRICT REQUIREMENTS (READ CAREFULLY)



Do NOT apply if you do not meet ALL of the following:



You have personally handled at least 200–300 chargebacks/disputes end-to-end (not just “assisted”)



You can demonstrate a minimum win rate of 60%+ (or provide proof/results from past role)



Strong working experience with Stripe + PayPal (Shopify Payments is a big plus)



You understand dispute timelines, evidence standards, reason codes, and how processors evaluate cases



You are highly organized and can manage high-volume environments without missing deadlines



Excellent written English — clear, direct, professional rebuttals



Nice to have (bonus):



Shopify admin knowledge (order timelines, fulfillment, policies, customer notes, tags)



Experience improving dispute ratio through operational improvements (shipping, policy clarity, CX scripts)



Prior team lead or manager experience in dispute/risk ops



WORK SETUP



Independent Contractor (remote)



Full-time: 8 hours/day, 40 hours/week



Flexible schedule, but you must meet deadlines and be responsive



We require Hubstaff time tracking: 



COMPENSATION



Hourly rate based on proven experience and results



Performance incentives possible (based on win rate, dispute ratio improvement, team output/quality)



APPLICATION PROCESS (STRICT FILTER)



To apply, send the answers below. Applications without these will be ignored.



How many disputes/chargebacks have you personally handled end-to-end? (number)



What is your historical win rate? Provide proof/screenshot or a breakdown by processor if possible.



Which processors have you worked with? (Stripe, PayPal, Shopify Payments, others)



What are the top 3 dispute reason codes you handled most, and how did you win them?



Describe your evidence checklist for “Item Not Received” and “Fraud/Unauthorized”.



Have you ever built SOPs and trained agents? If yes, explain briefly.



What is your hourly rate expectation?



Optional practical test (shortlisted):

We may send 3 sample cases to draft rebuttals for (paid test for shortlisted candidates).

title: Claude AI Technical Specialist
description:
* Starting at $950/month (may vary based on experience)



Job Summary



We are looking for a highly skilled Claude AI Technical Specialist who has hands-on, real-world experience deploying Claude AI solutions for businesses. This is NOT an entry-level position. We require demonstrated, verifiable experience using Claude (paid/professional tier) to solve business problems, automate workflows, and deliver measurable outcomes for companies.

You will be responsible for designing, building, and managing AI-powered workflows, automations, and integrations using Claude as the core engine. You must be able to articulate what you built, why it worked, and what results it produced



Key Responsibilities

* Design and implement Claude AI-powered workflows, automations, and integrations for business use cases.

* Build and optimize prompts, prompt chains, and multi-step AI pipelines for reliable, production-grade outputs

* Integrate Claude via API into business tools (CRMs, helpdesks, internal dashboards, communication platforms)

*Identify automation opportunities within client or company operations and propose AI-driven solutions

*Document processes, prompt logic, and deployment architecture clearly for team handoff

*Monitor AI outputs for quality, accuracy, and performance; iterate and improve continuously

*Train and guide non-technical tea
mbers on how to use and interact with AI tools effectively

*Stay current with Claude model updates, new capabilities, and Anthropic product releases



Technical Skills:

Claude AI & Prompt Engineering

•Advanced prompt engineering: system prompts, few-shot examples, chain-of-thought, XML structuring

•Experience with Claude's API (claude-sonnet, claude-opus model families)

•Knowledge of context window management and multi-turn conversation design

•Ability to evaluate and compare Claude model versions for specific tasks



Integrations & Automation

•API integration experience 

•Automation platforms 

•Familiarity with tools like Hubspot, Salesforce etc.



Technical Foundations

•Comfortable working with JavaScript, Python, or no-code platforms to connect Claude to business tools

•Ability to read and write basic scripts for API calls, data parsing, and output formatting

•Understanding of data privacy, prompt injection risks, and responsible AI deployment

title: Coordination Specialist
description:
We are a growing real estate investment company looking for a motivated Virtual Assistant (VA) to help with lead generation and acquisitions for off-market properties. This role is ideal for someone who is confident speaking with homeowners, organized, and driven by performance-based commissions.



You will be responsible for contacting property owners, identifying motivated sellers, gathering property details, and nurturing leads until they are ready for a contract. Once the seller is ready to move forward, the deal will be handed off to our admin team for contract processing.



This position offers weekly pay plus a 5% commission on every deal that closes from your leads, giving you strong earning potential.





Responsibilities





Reach out to property owners through calls, SMS,
, or other outreach methods



Identify homeowners who may be interested in selling their property at a discounted price



Build rapport with potential sellers and determine their motivation and timeline



Gather important property information including:



Property condition



Photos of the property



Ownership details



Property history



Reason for selling



Enter and update information in the CRM



Follow up with leads regularly until they are ready to move forward



Coordinate with the internal team once a seller is ready for a purchase agreement



Maintain organized records of all conversations and leads





Requirements





Strong English communication skills (spoken and written)



Comfortable speaking with homeowners on the phone



Experience in real estate, cold calling, or lead generation preferred



Familiarity with CRM systems is a plus



Reliable internet connection and quiet workspace



Ability to work full-time during U.S. business hours



Self-motivated and results-driven

title: Remote SDR – Outbound Sales Rep & Closer
description:
Title: Virtual Assistant – Sales Development Rep 

Type: Remote, offshore, full time (US Pacific hours overlap required)

Reports to: Sales Manager, Y Media Services



Mission:

Generate new advertising clients for a local marketing agency (print, digital, TV, direct mail) by prospecting local businesses, booking qualified meetings, and closing them into monthly marketing packages.



Key Responsibilities

Research and build targeted lists of local businesses (restaurants, home services, health/wellness, retail, professional services, etc.).



Execute a structured outbound cadence (calls,
, light social) to business owners and decision makers.



Set qualified appointments for marketing consultations and, where appropriate, run those consultations yourself via phone/video.



Conduct brief discovery: understand the business, their local marketing, and customer growth goals.



Present and explain Y Media’s advertising solutions (print, magazine, digital, TV, direct mail) using provided decks, rate sheets, and scripts.



Close new clients into clear packages (typically recurring monthly campaigns) following defined pricing and terms.



Follow up with all warm leads and no shows to re book and move them to a decision.



Keep the CRM fully updated: activities, notes, opportunity stages, deals closed.



Hit and exceed weekly/monthly KPIs for dials, conversations, appointments, and closed deals.



Daily Activities

Make a targeted number of outbound calls to new and existing leads each day.



Send personalized follow up and nurture
s using templates and your own wording.



Run short discovery/closing calls for interested prospects and present offers.



Confirm upcoming appointments with the CEO or yourself (depending on the process).



Log all work in the CRM and prepare simple daily and weekly performance reports.



Requirements

1–2+ years' experience in outbound sales, SDR, appointment setting, or closing (phone/Zoom).



Strong spoken and written English with a confident, friendly phone presence.



Comfortable handling objections and asking for the sale in a professional, respectful way.



Experience using CRM and online tools (e.g., pipeline tracking,
, calendar).



Ability to follow scripts and processes while sounding natural and adapting to each prospect.



Reliable internet connection, headset, and quiet working environment.



Self?motivated, organized, and able to manage your time and pipeline with minimal supervision.



Personality & Work Style

Hungry and results driven; enjoys seeing numbers and commissions grow.



Resilient and persistent with outbound calling and follow up.



Coachable, open to call reviews and feedback, willing to practice and improve.



Honest, reliable, and interested in a long term role with growing responsibility.



Compensation

Base: Lower fixed base (e.g., in the ~$500–$600/month range, depending on experience).



Commission: Tiered commission on every new client you close, with higher commission rates as you close more deals in a month.



Upside: Realistic total monthly earnings can reach around ~$1,200+ for consistent performance, with no cap on commission.



(Exact numbers and tiers will be shared during the interview and in the contract.)



How to Apply

Send your CV or a short voice recording (30–60 seconds) introducing yourself and sharing your outbound sales experience. In the subject of your application, please include YM575



Include a brief note on your past results (e.g., appointments booked/month, deals closed/month) and your available working hours in Pacific Time.

title: Car Rental Sales & Customer Service
description:
We are a Michigan-based car rental and auto repair company seeking a **highly reliable and detail-oriented virtual assistant** to handle customer inquiries, rental coordination, and problem resolution. You’ll work directly with customers who want to rent vehicles or are experiencing issues with their current rental.



This is a **full-time role (50 hours/week)**. You must be available to work **5 days per week, including Saturdays and Sundays**, with the ability to work as late as **10 PM Eastern Time**.



### Responsibilities:



* Respond promptly to inbound customer requests (calls, texts, and messages) regarding car rentals

* Troubleshoot rental issues (e.g., car won’t start, flat tire) and coordinate roadside assistance or towing

* Coordinate maintenance or repair needs with our auto repair team

* Schedule vehicles and update customer records

* Follow up with renters and maintain accurate communication logs



### Qualifications:



* Excellent spoken and written English

* Strong problem-solving and critical thinking skills

* Experience in customer service, preferably with cars, rentals, or logistics

* High-speed internet, noise-canceling headset, and a quiet workspace

* Tech-savvy and able to learn new tools quickly (e.g., CRM, text/call software, GPS tracking systems)



---



### **IMPORTANT – HOW TO APPLY:**



To show us you pay attention to detail and follow instructions, **please include the word “watermelon” in your subject line or response title**.



At the **very top** of your application, answer all of the following:



1. What time zone you’re located in

2. What your expected hourly rate is (in USD)

3. What days/hours you are available to work (please confirm weekend availability)

4. Send me a recording discussing your work history starting in 2018. Start with 2018 and discuss where you worked, what the company did, and what your role in the company was and then why you left, and then move forward in time.



**Applications without these details or the word “watermelon” will be ignored.**

title: Paid Ads Manager (Meta & Google)
description:
Luxury Beauty Brands | Performance-Driven | Strategy-First



About the Role



We are looking for a high-level Paid Ads Manager to own and scale paid advertising across a growing beauty brand ecosystem, including:



Aavya Hair Studio – Luxury hair extension salon (local lead generation)



Aavya Hair – Premium raw hair extension e-commerce brand



Hair Launch Academy – Coaching programs, webinars, and live trainings for beauty professionals



This is not a plug-and-play role.



We are looking for someone who thinks strategically, understands consumer psychology, funnels, testing frameworks, and performance optimization, and can make smart decisions without constant supervision.



If you need step-by-step instructions for every campaign, this role is not the right fit.



Key Responsibilities

Paid Advertising Strategy & Execution



Plan, launch, and scale Meta 
 & Instagram) and Google Ads campaigns



Develop custom strategies for each brand (local services, e-commerce, and webinar funnels)



Select the right campaign objectives, audiences, and creative angles



Build structured testing frameworks for creatives, copy, audiences, offers, and landing pages



Continuously optimize campaigns based on real performance data



Funnel & Conversion Optimization



Understand the entire marketing funnel:

Ad ? Landing Page ? Booking / Checkout / Webinar



Work alongside automation and CRM systems (GoHighLevel preferred)



Identify conversion drop-off points and recommend improvements



Collaborate on landing page testing and offer optimization



Creative Direction (Beyond Just Running Ads)



Provide clear creative direction for ad production (hooks, angles, formats, UGC styles)



Identify and scale winning creatives



Understand what performs in beauty, luxury, and education markets



Test a variety of formats: short-form video, static, carousel, storytelling ads



Data, Testing & Performance Optimization



Monitor performance metrics daily:



CPL



CPA



ROAS



CTR



Conversion Rate



Scale winners and cut underperforming campaigns quickly



Understand attribution models and platform data differences



Deliver clear performance reports with actionable insights



What We're Looking For (Non-Negotiable)



You must:



Have proven experience managing profitable Meta and Google Ads campaigns



Understand local service ads, e-commerce, and webinar funnels



Be comfortable testing, analyzing, and making decisions independently



Know how to interpret data and adjust strategy without waiting for instructions



Be proactive, organized, and detail-oriented



Communicate clearly and professionally



Be capable of managing multiple ad accounts and brands simultaneously



Strong Preference If You



Have experience with beauty, salons, e-commerce, or coaching programs



Have worked with GoHighLevel, Shopify, Stripe, or booking calendars



Understand luxury brand positioning and premium pricing



Have scaled campaigns beyond starter-level budgets



What Success Looks Like in This Role



Salon campaigns consistently generate qualified booked consultations



E-commerce campaigns drive profitable sales and repeat customers



Coaching campaigns fill webinars, live trainings, and high-ticket programs



Testing frameworks are consistent and data-driven



You bring strategic ideas, new angles, and growth opportunities



Reporting & Communication



Weekly performance reports covering:



What worked



What didn’t



What will be tested next



Clear recommendations for scaling or pivoting campaigns



Strong ownership mindset — you treat ad spend like it's your own money



Compensation



Competitive monthly retainer (based on experience)



Performance bonuses for strong results



Long-term opportunity managing multiple brands with growing ad budgets



Fully remote position



How to Apply



To be considered, please include:



A brief overview of your paid ads experience



Examples of campaigns you have managed (industry, objective, results)



Your approach to testing and scaling campaigns



Why you believe you’re a strong fit for luxury beauty and education brands



Applicants who submit generic applications will not be considered.



Final Note



We are building serious brands with serious revenue goals.



This role is for a high-level media buyer who wants ownership, autonomy, and the opportunity to scale impactful brands.



If you’re strategic, analytical, and performance-driven — we want to hear from you.



Please send your CV and application to:

title: B2B Lead Generation VA — Cold Email & Outreach
description:
ABOUT THE COMPANY

SPOR Group is a UK-based AV technology company. We design, install, and manage meeting room technology for enterprise clients across the UK, Europe, and the US. We manage 1,500+ meeting rooms for 200+ companies and run a proprietary remote monitoring platform called SPORTrack.

We work with large organisations — think 500–5,000 person companies where the IT Director or Facilities Manager is responsible for making sure meetings actually work. That's our client.

We're growing fast and we need a detail-oriented, proactive VA to own our outbound lead generation operation week in, week out.



THE ROLE

This is a B2B lead generation role. Your job is to keep our cold outreach pipeline full and running smoothly so that our founder can focus on calls and closing deals.

You will NOT be doing the selling. You will be building the lists, setting up the sequences, managing replies in the CRM, and making sure the whole system runs like clockwork every week.

This is a long-term role with room to grow. If you're good at this, we'll give you more responsibility and more hours over time.



WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING

List Building (weekly)



Search Wiza for new prospects matching our ICP (enterprise Facilities Managers and IT Directors at 500–5,000 person companies in the UK and Europe)

Export and clean prospect lists (100–200 contacts per week)

Run all
 addresses through a verification tool (NeverBounce or ZeroBounce) before import

Remove duplicates and cross-check against existing HubSpot contacts



Sequence Management (weekly)



Import verified lists into Instantly.ai and enrol them in the correct
 sequence

Monitor open rates, reply rates, and bounce rates — flag anything that looks off

Pause sequences or individual contacts as directed



CRM Management (weekly)



Log all new contacts in HubSpot with the correct source, tags, and properties

Update contact stages when replies come in (Leads ? Appointments ? Presentations)

Create follow-up tasks for the founder when positive replies arrive

Keep the pipeline clean — no stale contacts, no missing data



LinkedIn Outreach (weekly)



Send 20–25 connection requests per day to ICP prospects

Monitor who has accepted and log in HubSpot

Send follow-up DMs using provided templates (with light personalisation)



Trigger Event Research (weekly)



Monitor Google Alerts and LinkedIn for office move announcements, new senior hires, and AV-related job postings at target companies

Compile a short weekly report of the top 5–10 priority targets and why



Reporting (weekly)



Send a brief weekly summary every Friday:
s sent, open rate, reply rate, new leads added to HubSpot, LinkedIn connections sent/accepted





WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

Must have:



At least 1 year of experience in B2B lead generation or outbound sales support

Hands-on experience with Wiza or a similar prospecting tool (Hunter, Lusha, etc.)

Familiarity with cold
 sequencing tools (Instantly, Lemlist, Woodpecker, or similar)

Experience using a CRM — HubSpot preferred, but Salesforce or Pipedrive is fine

Strong English — written and verbal. You'll be writing personalised outreach messages.

Extremely organised and self-directed. This role runs on systems and consistency.

Reliable internet connection and dedicated workspace



Nice to have:



Experience in AV, IT, facilities management, or B2B tech sectors

Familiarity with LinkedIn Sales Navigator

Experience with Clay.com or other enrichment tools

Previous work with a UK or US-based company





WHAT THIS JOB IS NOT



This is not a customer service role

This is not a social media management role

This is not a data entry role where you just follow a checklist blindly — we need someone who thinks and flags problems





HOURS AND SCHEDULE



Part-time to start: 20 hours per week

Flexible schedule, but you must be available for a weekly check-in call (UK time, typically Monday morning)

Potential to move to full-time as the role grows

Long-term position — we are not looking for someone for a one-off project





PAY



Starting rate: $5–$8 USD per hour depending on experience

Reviewed after 90 days





HOW TO APPLY

Please do not send a generic application. We will not read it.

Instead, answer these three questions in your application message:



What prospecting or sequencing tools have you used before, and what did you use each one for?

Describe a lead generation process you've run before — what was the ICP, how did you build the list, and what results did it produce?

What's your process for keeping a CRM clean and up to date?



Applications that don't answer these questions will not be considered.



A NOTE FROM THE FOUNDER

I'm Chris. I run SPOR Group. I'm looking for someone I can trust to own this process and run it without me having to chase them. If you're the kind of person who takes initiative, spots problems before they become disasters, and takes pride in doing things properly — we'll get along fine.

I'm not looking for perfect. I'm looking for reliable, smart, and hungry to learn.

title: Casting & Content Creation
description:

If we work well together, I have marketing tasks as well. Thank you.



Please send a resume and cover letter to 
 you!

title: Remote Dental Financial Coordinator
description:
Brite Orthodontics is dedicated to creating beautiful smiles and lasting relationships! Compliance & Accuracy

Reporting & Analysis

description:

Web site Building

This role will create multimedia content that highlights professional expertise, wellness, style, and empowerment while building a strong digital presence across social media platforms.







The ideal candidate is creative, organized, and knowledgeable about social media trends, storytelling, and digital marketing while maintaining professionalism and credibility in healthcare-related messaging.

preventative health



healthcare leadership



nurse practitioner insights



patient education



wellness trends







Brand Building
